seoTitle: IXYS Products | Power MOSFETs, IGBTs, Diodes | LiTong Distributor
seoDescription: Explore IXYS product portfolio including power MOSFETs, IGBT modules, fast recovery diodes, and thyristors. Authorized distributor with technical support and selection guidance.

faqs.question: What are the main product categories offered by IXYS?
faqs.answer: IXYS offers four main power semiconductor product categories: (1) Power MOSFETs - high-voltage and low-voltage MOSFETs for switching applications with industry-leading on-resistance; (2) IGBT Modules - insulated gate bipolar transistors for medium to high power motor drives and power supplies; (3) Power Diodes - fast recovery and standard recovery diodes for rectification, freewheeling, and snubber applications; (4) Thyristors - silicon controlled rectifiers (SCRs) and triacs for phase control and AC power switching. Each category features multiple series optimized for specific voltage, current, and switching requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review each product category to find the IXYS solution matching your power electronics requirements.

faqs.question: How do I select the right IXYS power semiconductor for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right IXYS power semiconductor involves several key considerations: (1) Voltage rating - choose Vds or Vce at least 20-30% higher than maximum operating voltage; (2) Current rating - select continuous current rating based on thermal constraints and peak current requirements; (3) Switching frequency - for high-frequency applications (>100kHz), consider MOSFETs; for lower frequencies, IGBTs may be more efficient; (4) Thermal requirements - evaluate Rth and maximum junction temperature for your cooling solution; (5) Package - select TO-220, TO-247, D2PAK, or modules based on power dissipation and mounting requirements. Contact LiTong FAE for detailed selection guidance and thermal modeling.
faqs.decisionGuide: Match voltage/current ratings to your application with adequate margins. Contact FAE for thermal analysis and selection support.

faqs.question: What are the key advantages of IXYS power MOSFETs?
faqs.answer: IXYS power MOSFETs offer several competitive advantages: (1) Low on-resistance (Rds(on)) - enabling lower conduction losses and higher efficiency; (2) Fast switching speed - reducing switching losses in high-frequency applications; (3) High voltage capability - products available from 40V to 1000V+ for various applications; (4) Robust avalanche capability - many devices rated for unclamped inductive switching; (5) Excellent thermal performance - low thermal resistance packages for effective heat dissipation; (6) Wide product range - from small-signal to high-power devices in various packages. IXYS MOSFETs are particularly strong in high-voltage applications above 500V.
faqs.decisionGuide: IXYS MOSFETs are ideal for high-voltage, high-frequency switching applications requiring low losses and reliable performance.

faqs.question: How do IXYS IGBT modules compare to discrete IGBTs?
faqs.answer: IXYS IGBT modules offer significant advantages over discrete IGBTs for higher power applications: (1) Integrated design - modules include IGBT chips, freewheeling diodes, and thermal management in one package; (2) Higher power density - modules can handle hundreds of amps in compact packages; (3) Better thermal performance - direct bond copper (DBC) substrate provides excellent heat spreading; (4) Simplified assembly - reduces component count and assembly time; (5) Improved reliability - factory-tested modules ensure consistent performance. IXYS offers modules in various configurations including half-bridge, full-bridge, and three-phase topologies for motor drives, inverters, and power supplies.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose IGBT modules for applications above 50A; discrete IGBTs are suitable for lower current applications.

faqs.question: What makes IXYS fast recovery diodes suitable for high-frequency applications?
faqs.answer: IXYS fast recovery diodes are optimized for high-frequency power electronics with: (1) Fast reverse recovery time (trr) - typically 25-100ns for minimal switching losses; (2) Low reverse recovery charge (Qrr) - reducing EMI and switching stress on associated devices; (3) Soft recovery characteristics - minimizing voltage overshoots and ringing; (4) High surge current capability - handling inrush currents in power supplies; (5) Wide voltage range - available from 100V to 2000V+ for various applications. These characteristics make IXYS diodes ideal for output rectification in SMPS, freewheeling in motor drives, and snubber applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: IXYS fast recovery diodes are ideal for high-frequency switching applications requiring minimal reverse recovery losses.

categories.name: Power MOSFETs
categories.description: IXYS power MOSFETs offer industry-leading performance for switching applications with low on-resistance and fast switching characteristics.
categories.longDescription: IXYS power MOSFETs deliver exceptional performance for power conversion and switching applications. Featuring advanced trench and planar technologies, these MOSFETs offer ultra-low on-resistance, fast switching speeds, and robust avalanche capability. As an authorized IXYS distributor, LiTong provides comprehensive selection guidance for power MOSFETs ranging from 40V to 1000V+. The portfolio includes devices optimized for high-frequency SMPS, motor drives, DC-DC converters, and automotive applications. IXYS MOSFETs are available in industry-standard packages including TO-220, TO-247, D2PAK, and surface-mount options.

selectionGuide.title: IXYS Power MOSFET Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide to selecting the right IXYS MOSFET for your power electronics application

series.name: TrenchFET Series
series.description: Low-voltage MOSFETs with ultra-low Rds(on) for high-current applications

faqs.question: What is the difference between trench and planar MOSFET technologies?
faqs.answer: Trench and planar MOSFETs differ in structure and performance characteristics: (1) Trench MOSFETs - vertical channel structure provides higher cell density, lower Rds(on), and better for low-voltage (<200V) applications; (2) Planar MOSFETs - horizontal channel structure offers better high-voltage capability (>500V) and avalanche ruggedness. IXYS offers both technologies: TrenchFET series for low-voltage high-current applications, X-Class series for high-voltage applications. Trench devices typically have lower gate charge for faster switching, while planar devices offer superior body diode performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose trench MOSFETs for low-voltage high-current; planar MOSFETs for high-voltage applications.

faqs.question: How do I calculate power dissipation in a MOSFET?
faqs.answer: MOSFET power dissipation consists of conduction and switching losses: (1) Conduction loss - Pcond = I² × Rds(on) × duty cycle. Use Rds(on) at actual operating temperature (typically 1.5-2x 25°C value); (2) Switching loss - Psw = 0.5 × V × I × (tr + tf) × fsw, where tr/tf are rise/fall times, fsw is switching frequency; (3) Gate drive loss - Pgate = Qg × Vgs × fsw; (4) Total loss - Ptotal = Pcond + Psw + Pgate. Ensure total dissipation is within thermal limits: Tj = Ta + (Ptotal × Rth(j-a)). IXYS datasheets provide thermal and switching characteristics for accurate calculations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate both conduction and switching losses at worst-case conditions to ensure reliable operation.

faqs.question: What is avalanche rating and why is it important?
faqs.answer: Avalanche rating (EAS - Single Pulse Avalanche Energy) indicates a MOSFET's ability to withstand unclamped inductive switching events: (1) Definition - energy the device can absorb when switching off an inductive load without external clamping; (2) Importance - protects against voltage transients in motor drives, solenoids, and relay applications; (3) Rating - specified in millijoules (mJ) at specific current and starting temperature; (4) IXYS advantage - many IXYS MOSFETs feature robust avalanche capability, eliminating need for external protection components. Always ensure EAS rating exceeds worst-case energy in your application.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select MOSFETs with adequate avalanche rating for inductive load applications to eliminate external protection.

faqs.question: How does temperature affect MOSFET performance?
faqs.answer: Temperature significantly impacts MOSFET characteristics: (1) Rds(on) increases with temperature - typically 0.3-0.5% per °C, meaning 50-80% higher at 125°C vs 25°C; (2) Gate threshold voltage decreases - approximately -7mV/°C; (3) Switching speed may decrease slightly at high temperatures; (4) Maximum current decreases due to higher thermal resistance. Design considerations: (1) Size for worst-case Rds(on) at maximum junction temperature; (2) Ensure adequate heatsinking for continuous operation; (3) Consider derating for high ambient temperatures. IXYS datasheets provide normalized curves for temperature effects.
faqs.decisionGuide: Always design using maximum Rds(on) at worst-case junction temperature, not the 25°C datasheet value.

faqs.question: What gate drive considerations are important for IXYS MOSFETs?
faqs.answer: Proper gate drive is critical for MOSFET performance: (1) Drive voltage - use 10-15V for standard MOSFETs, 5V for logic-level devices; (2) Gate resistor - controls switching speed and EMI; typical values 5-50Ω depending on switching frequency; (3) Drive current - must charge/discharge gate capacitance quickly; Ig = Qg/t, where t is desired switching time; (4) Miller plateau - ensure drive can supply plateau current for fast switching; (5) Negative voltage - consider -3 to -5V for high-power applications to prevent false turn-on. IXYS provides recommended gate drive circuits in application notes.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design gate drive with adequate voltage, current capability, and proper gate resistance for your switching frequency.

products.name: 600V 40A Power MOSFET
products.shortDescription: High-voltage N-channel MOSFET with 600V rating and ultra-low 85mΩ on-resistance for power supplies and motor drives.
descriptionParagraphs: The IXFH40N60P3 is a high-performance 600V N-channel power MOSFET featuring advanced planar technology. With ultra-low 85mΩ maximum on-resistance and 40A continuous current capability, it delivers excellent efficiency for power conversion applications.

This MOSFET features robust avalanche capability with 500mJ single-pulse avalanche energy rating, eliminating the need for external protection in many inductive load applications. The TO-247 package provides excellent thermal performance with low junction-to-case thermal resistance.
Ideal for high-power SMPS, motor drives, inverters, and power factor correction circuits where high efficiency and reliability are critical requirements.
features: Fast switching characteristics reduce switching losses
Low gate charge for efficient gate drive
TO-247 package with excellent thermal performance
Avalanche rated for unclamped inductive switching

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Electronics
faeReview.content: The IXFH40N60P3 is my go-to recommendation for 600V applications requiring 30-40A capability. In my experience designing industrial motor drives, this MOSFET delivers excellent thermal performance and reliability. The 85mΩ Rds(on) is competitive with newer superjunction devices, while the planar technology offers superior avalanche ruggedness. I particularly appreciate the consistent performance across temperature - the Rds(on) stays well-controlled even at high junction temperatures. For applications with inductive loads, the 500mJ avalanche rating often eliminates the need for external protection circuits, simplifying design and reducing BOM cost. I recommend using 15V gate drive for fastest switching and lowest losses.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent 600V MOSFET with robust avalanche capability for industrial motor drives and power supplies

alternativeParts.reason: Lower current rating for reduced power applications

companionParts.description: Fast recovery diode for freewheeling applications

faqs.question: What is the maximum operating frequency for this MOSFET?
faqs.answer: The IXFH40N60P3 can operate at switching frequencies up to 500kHz in hard-switching applications, and up to 1MHz in resonant or soft-switching topologies. At 100kHz switching frequency with 300V bus voltage and 20A current, switching losses are approximately 15-20W. For high-frequency operation above 200kHz, consider using resonant topologies or zero-voltage switching to minimize switching losses. The gate charge of 110nC requires adequate gate drive current - recommend using gate drivers with 2A+ source/sink capability for fast switching.
faqs.decisionGuide: Suitable for applications up to 500kHz; use soft-switching for frequencies above 200kHz.

faqs.question: What heatsink is required for 30A continuous operation?
faqs.answer: For 30A continuous operation with the IXFH40N60P3: (1) Conduction loss at 30A = 30² × 0.085 = 76.5W (using max Rds(on)); (2) Allowing for switching losses of ~10W at moderate frequencies, total dissipation ~85-90W; (3) Maximum junction temperature 150°C, ambient 40°C, allowable rise 110°C; (4) Required thermal resistance Rth(sa) = 110°C / 90W = 1.22°C/W or better. Recommended heatsinks: Profile heatsinks with forced air cooling (2-3 m/s), or extruded heatsinks with natural convection for lower currents. Always use thermal interface material with 0.1-0.3°C·cm²/W thermal resistance. Monitor case temperature to verify thermal design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use heatsink with Rth(sa) < 1.2°C/W with forced air for 30A continuous operation.

faqs.question: Can this MOSFET be used in bridge configurations?
faqs.answer: Yes, the IXFH40N60P3 is well-suited for bridge configurations including half-bridge, full-bridge, and three-phase bridges. Key considerations: (1) Body diode - the integral body diode has typical 250ns reverse recovery time; add external fast recovery diodes in parallel for high-frequency applications; (2) Dead time - implement 200-500ns dead time to prevent shoot-through; (3) Gate drive - use isolated gate drivers for high-side switches with proper bootstrap or isolated power supplies; (4) Layout - minimize loop inductance between bridge legs to reduce voltage overshoots. For motor drive applications up to 5kW, this MOSFET provides excellent performance and reliability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Excellent for bridge topologies; add external freewheeling diodes for high-frequency applications.

faqs.question: What is the recommended gate drive voltage?
faqs.answer: The recommended gate drive voltage for IXFH40N60P3 is 10-15V for optimal performance: (1) 10V drive - provides good conduction with Rds(on) near datasheet values; (2) 12V drive - recommended for most applications, provides margin against drive voltage variations; (3) 15V drive - optimal for lowest Rds(on) and fastest switching, but increases gate drive losses; (4) Below 8V - not recommended as Rds(on) increases significantly. The gate threshold voltage is 3-5V, so 10V+ provides adequate margin. For high-reliability applications, consider using 12-15V drive with undervoltage lockout protection on the gate driver.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 12-15V gate drive for optimal performance; never operate below 8V gate voltage.

faqs.question: How does this MOSFET compare to superjunction devices?
faqs.answer: Compared to superjunction MOSFETs, the IXFH40N60P3 planar technology offers different trade-offs: (1) Rds(on) - superjunction devices have lower Rds(on) per die area (e.g., 60mΩ vs 85mΩ for similar price); (2) Switching - superjunction devices typically have higher dV/dt and may require snubbers; (3) Avalanche - planar devices like IXFH40N60P3 have superior avalanche ruggedness; (4) EMI - planar devices often produce less EMI due to softer switching characteristics; (5) Cost - planar devices are typically more cost-effective. For applications requiring avalanche capability or with inductive loads, the IXFH40N60P3 is often the better choice despite slightly higher Rds(on).
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose IXFH40N60P3 for avalanche ruggedness; consider superjunction for lowest Rds(on) in non-inductive applications.

products.name: 150V 96A Trench MOSFET
products.shortDescription: Low-voltage N-channel trench MOSFET with 150V rating and ultra-low 6.5mΩ on-resistance for high-current DC-DC converters.
descriptionParagraphs: The IXTP96N15T is a high-performance 150V N-channel trench MOSFET designed for high-current applications. Featuring ultra-low 6.5mΩ maximum on-resistance and 96A continuous current capability, it delivers exceptional efficiency for DC-DC conversion.

Advanced trench technology enables high cell density and low gate charge (Qg = 155nC), allowing fast switching with reduced gate drive losses. The TO-220 package provides cost-effective thermal management for medium power applications.
Ideal for synchronous rectification in server power supplies, battery chargers, and high-current DC-DC converters where low conduction losses are critical.
features: High 96A current capability for high-power applications
Trench technology for fast switching performance
Low gate charge reduces gate drive requirements

faeReview.author: David Liu
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - Power Systems
faeReview.content: The IXTP96N15T is an excellent choice for synchronous rectification in high-current applications. I've used this MOSFET in multiple 48V server power supply designs with outstanding results. The 6.5mΩ Rds(on) is very competitive, and the trench technology provides excellent switching performance. At 60A continuous current, conduction losses are only about 23W - significantly lower than competing devices. The TO-220 package is cost-effective and easy to heatsink. One tip: use a good thermal interface material and ensure adequate airflow - at high currents, every degree of temperature reduction helps. For synchronous rectification, pair with a fast gate driver to minimize body diode conduction time.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent low-voltage MOSFET for high-current synchronous rectification applications

alternativeParts.reason: Lower current rating for reduced power applications
Higher voltage and current rating with lower Rds(on)
alternativeParts.useCase: Applications requiring higher voltage margin or current

companionParts.description: Matching MOSFET for synchronous rectifier pair
Fast recovery diode for output rectification

faqs.question: What is the optimal application for this MOSFET?
faqs.answer: The IXTP96N15T is optimized for low-voltage high-current applications, particularly synchronous rectification in DC-DC converters and SMPS. Its ultra-low 6.5mΩ Rds(on) minimizes conduction losses at high currents, making it ideal for: (1) 48V server power supplies - excellent for 12V output synchronous rectifiers; (2) Battery chargers - high efficiency at high charging currents; (3) Telecom rectifiers - 48V to intermediate bus conversion; (4) Motor drives - low-voltage high-current motor control. The 150V rating provides adequate margin for 48V systems with transients. For applications above 75A continuous, ensure adequate heatsinking as conduction losses become significant.
faqs.decisionGuide: Best for synchronous rectification in 48V systems and high-current DC-DC converters.

faqs.question: How do I minimize switching losses in high-frequency applications?
faqs.answer: To minimize switching losses with IXTP96N15T at high frequencies: (1) Gate drive - use low-impedance gate driver (2A+ capability) with proper gate resistor (5-10Ω); (2) Drive voltage - use 10-12V gate drive for optimal switching speed; (3) Layout - minimize gate loop inductance (<10nH) and drain-source loop inductance; (4) Snubbers - consider RC snubbers if voltage overshoot exceeds 80% of Vds rating; (5) Dead time - in bridge configurations, minimize dead time to reduce body diode conduction. At 100kHz, switching losses are typically 10-15W. For frequencies above 200kHz, consider using resonant topologies or advanced soft-switching techniques to minimize switching losses.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use strong gate drive and optimize layout for minimal switching losses at high frequencies.

faqs.question: What is the maximum power dissipation without a heatsink?
faqs.answer: Without a heatsink, the IXTP96N15T in TO-220 package has limited power handling: (1) Thermal resistance junction-to-air Rth(j-a) is approximately 62°C/W for free air mounting; (2) Maximum junction temperature is 175°C; (3) At 25°C ambient, maximum power dissipation = (175-25)/62 = 2.4W; (4) At 50°C ambient, maximum power = (175-50)/62 = 2.0W. This limits continuous current to approximately 19A at 25°C ambient (P = I² × Rds(on) = 19² × 0.0065 = 2.35W). For higher currents, a heatsink is mandatory. Even a small heatsink (Rth = 20°C/W) increases capability to 6W, supporting 30A continuous operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use heatsink for currents above 15A; free-air operation limited to ~2W dissipation.

faqs.question: Can this MOSFET be paralleled for higher current?
faqs.answer: Yes, the IXTP96N15T can be paralleled for higher current applications: (1) Current sharing - use individual gate resistors (1-2Ω) for each MOSFET to prevent oscillations; (2) Layout - ensure symmetric layout with equal trace lengths and impedances to each device; (3) Matching - devices from same production batch typically match within 10% for Rds(on); (4) Temperature - thermal coupling helps balance currents as Rds(on) increases with temperature; (5) Gate drive - use single gate driver with sufficient current capability (4A+ for two devices). Two IXTP96N15T in parallel can handle 150-170A continuous with proper heatsinking. Always verify current sharing with current probes during prototype testing.
faqs.decisionGuide: Can be paralleled with proper gate resistors and symmetric layout for 150A+ applications.

faqs.question: What are the key layout considerations for this MOSFET?
faqs.answer: Proper PCB layout is critical for IXTP96N15T performance: (1) Gate drive loop - minimize loop area between gate driver and MOSFET gate/source; keep traces short and wide; (2) Power loops - minimize inductance in high di/dt paths; use copper planes for drain and source connections; (3) Kelvin connection - use separate source connection for gate drive return to avoid source inductance feedback; (4) Decoupling - place ceramic capacitors (100nF-1µF) close to drain and source terminals; (5) Thermal vias - use thermal vias under the drain pad to spread heat to inner layers. Poor layout can cause voltage overshoots, oscillations, and increased switching losses. Follow IXYS application notes for recommended layouts.
faqs.decisionGuide: Minimize gate and power loop inductance; use Kelvin source connection and adequate thermal vias.
